provider.name: Ministry of MSME (National SC-ST Hub, implemented by NSIC)

benefits.name: Financial incentives for SC/ST MSEs (reimbursements and procurement support)
benefits.summary: A set of financial incentives to help SC/ST-owned MSEs participate in public procurement and build capacity, including reimbursements for e-commerce membership, training and certification, and support linked to the special SC/ST capital subsidy.
benefits.calculation_logic: Includes reimbursement of e-commerce portal membership fees (for example GeM, TRIFED) at 80pct or maximum 25,000 per financial year, reimbursement of top-institute short-course fees, and other procurement-participation support. Linked to SCLCSS (25pct capital subsidy, captured in the Ministry of MSME batch).

metadata.notes: Bundles several small financial incentives for SC/ST MSEs. Max shown is the e-commerce membership reimbursement cap; other components (course-fee and certification reimbursements) have separate caps to confirm. SCLCSS is the linked capital subsidy.

suggested_faqs.question: What is the main objective of National SC-ST Hub (NSSH)?
suggested_faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide assistance and support under National SC-ST Hub (NSSH) to eligible beneficiaries.

suggested_faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for National SC-ST Hub (NSSH)?
suggested_faqs.answer: Businesses belonging to sc_st_entrepreneur, micro, small in manufacturing, services, sector_agnostic may apply.
